parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now answer the questions on your screen.

Speaker 2: Listen, why don't you and Kate come over for dinner sometime?

Speaker 1: That's a great idea. When?

Speaker 2: How about next Friday?

Speaker 1: Can't do Friday, I'm afraid. Both Kate and I have a parent-teacher afternoon on Friday after school. And then we'll have to do the paperwork in the evening. What about Saturday then?

Speaker 2: That would normally be fine, but our babysitter's away that night and we won't be able to find one at such short notice.

Speaker 1: Why don't you and Gordon come over for dinner at ours and then we'll be able to look after the kids? You'll be able to meet them too.

Speaker 2: Okay, let's do that then. Gordon will be happy. He won't have to wash up.

Speaker 1: I'll be doing the cooking when you come round. I love cooking. So what time shall we come round?

Speaker 2: Eight o'clock?

Speaker 1: Well, we'll be home around six that night as we're going out for the afternoon with the kids. I'd say come at seven o'clock.

Speaker 2: Are you sure that's long enough to get things ready?

Speaker 1: Well, that'll be an hour. I don't need more than that. And what's your phone number, just in case?

Speaker 2: 01347 835 825. That's the home number. And the mobile is 07978 290 512. What about you?

Speaker 1: I've only got a mobile. It's 07623 963 957.

Speaker 2: What was that last bit again?

Speaker 1: I'll say it all again. 07623 963 957. And I better give you the address.

Speaker 2: Oh yes, that would help.

Speaker 1: We live at flat 3, Evergreen Terrace. It's just past the church on the right. We've got a park entrance opposite us and a doctor's surgery next door.

Speaker 2: I should be able to find that. Anything you don't like to eat?

Speaker 1: Well, I hate fish and seafood, and Gordon doesn't care much for red meat. He likes white meat, though. How about chicken?

Speaker 2: That should be fine.

Speaker 1: Oh yes, when you arrive, none of the bells is marked for security and there are four buttons. You need to press the bell second from the bottom.

Speaker 2: Second from the bottom. OK, got that. I'll be able to buzz you in and then come up to the first floor and the door will be open.

Speaker 1: Great. Well, it's so good to see you again, Heath. I'll really look forward to seeing you on Saturday and meeting Kate again. And the kids, of course.

Speaker 2: Yes, and I'm looking forward to meeting Gordon. See you on Saturday then.

Speaker 1: Goodbye.

Speaker 2: Goodbye.
